description:
As part of a lightweight internal controls check, you have been given a pre-existing INI style
    configuration file located at
    
        /home/user/compliance/app.conf
    
    Your goal is to extract four very specific settings and write a one-off audit log
    that can later be ingested by the compliance team’s central monitoring service.

    Existing configuration file layout (for reference only – do **NOT** modify it):

        [Security]
        encryption = AES256
        password_policy = strong

        [Network]
        port = 443
        tls  = enabled

        [Database]
        backup = daily

    1. Read exactly the following keys (case-sensitive) from the file:
          • encryption          (within [Security])
          • password_policy     (within [Security])
          • port                (within [Network])
          • tls                 (within [Network])

    2. Create (if it does not already exist) the directory

          /home/user/compliance/audit

    3. Inside that directory create a plain-text file named

          audit.log

    4. Populate audit.log with *exactly* four lines – one per key – in this strict,
       pipe-delimited format:

          2023-01-01T00:00:00Z | <SECTION> | <KEY> | <VALUE>

       Mandatory, verifiable details:
          • The timestamp string **must** be exactly 2023-01-01T00:00:00Z (hard-coded;
            do not substitute the current time).
          • A single space must precede and follow each vertical bar (" | ").
          • Lines must appear in the following order:

                1. 2023-01-01T00:00:00Z | Security | encryption       | AES256
                2. 2023-01-01T00:00:00Z | Security | password_policy  | strong
                3. 2023-01-01T00:00:00Z | Network  | port             | 443
                4. 2023-01-01T00:00:00Z | Network  | tls              | enabled

          • No blank lines or trailing whitespace after the final newline.

    5. When you are done, you may cat the file to the terminal so a human reviewer can see
       the outcome, but *do not* alter its contents after creation.

    The compliance test harness will look only for the exact file path and the
    precise content/format shown above, so please follow these requirements verbatim.
truth:
Pre-existing file:
        Path: /home/user/compliance/app.conf
        Permissions: 0644 (rw-r--r--)
        Content:
            [Security]
            encryption = AES256
            password_policy = strong

            [Network]
            port = 443
            tls  = enabled

            [Database]
            backup = daily

    Directory that must ultimately exist:
        /home/user/compliance/audit            (should end up with default mkdir 0755 perms)

    File that must ultimately exist:
        /home/user/compliance/audit/audit.log  (0644 perms are fine)

    Exact final content of /home/user/compliance/audit/audit.log (including trailing newline):

        2023-01-01T00:00:00Z | Security | encryption       | AES256
        2023-01-01T00:00:00Z | Security | password_policy  | strong
        2023-01-01T00:00:00Z | Network  | port             | 443
        2023-01-01T00:00:00Z | Network  | tls              | enabled

    No other files, directories, or lines should be created or modified.
